patch.value: Audit domain: Docs, onboarding, release, changelog & CI

Audit docs/ (incl PRD, README, AGENTS.md, examples, RELEASING), changelog tooling (pm-changelog), CI workflows, plugins/marketplace, Claude Code/Codex plugins. Map coverage; surface docs/release/CI forward work + ADRs.

Domain audited read-only across code+docs+tests; existing pm coverage mapped via search; genuine gaps, forward work, improvements, ADRs, and agent/user stories proposed (dedup-checked); work-log + key decisions recorded in pm on this task.

value.author: audit-docs-agent
value.text: Progress 1/3: Read all docs/ files (QUICKSTART, AGENT_GUIDE, COMMANDS, CONFIGURATION, ARCHITECTURE, EXTENSIONS, SDK, TESTING, RELEASING, CODEX_PLUGIN, CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN, README, MIGRATION_CLI_SIMPLIFICATION, examples/). Read root files: README.md, AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (exists), marketplace.json, PR template, issue templates. CI workflows: ci.yml (5 gates: coverage/typecheck/static/compat/smokes + build matrix Ubuntu+macOS, Node 20-24), auto-release.yml (daily cron 02:35 UTC, dispatches release.yml), release.yml (tag-driven), nightly.yml (Node 20/22/24/25 + macOS + Windows), docs.yml (docs-only path filter). Key findings so far: (1) docs/README.md references CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md but no community link in README.md top-level nav; (2) CODEX_PLUGIN.md is very thin (33 lines) — barely a router; (3) marketplace.json only has pm-claude but not pm-codex; (4) no dedicated changelog-classifier config docs; (5) no docs/CHANGELOG_GUIDE.md for the auto-generated changelog workflow; (6) CI does not run on Windows in the PR matrix (only in nightly); (7) PR template does not mention AGENTS.md guidance update; (8) no ONBOARDING.md for new teammates.

value.author: audit-docs-agent
value.text: Progress 2/3: Deep-read release.yml, nightly.yml, docs.yml, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, PR template, bug-report/feature-request issue templates, dependabot.yml, pm-changelog classifyItem, docs/README guide topic map. Key additional findings: (1) README.md links to Claude Code plugin inline but no row in the 'Start Here' table for CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN.md -- asymmetry with CODEX_PLUGIN.md which does have its own table row; (2) docs/README.md 'Read Path' table has 'Codex user' row but no 'Claude Code user' row; (3) All 'Tracked documentation work' headers in docs/ still reference pm-1sb2/pm-3042/pm-r9gu (all closed) -- cosmetic stale refs; (4) PR template checklist is good but omits AGENTS.md guidance check when adding new agent-relevant commands; (5) changelog classifyItem logic is sound but entirely undocumented for contributors; (6) CI PR matrix: Ubuntu 20/22/24 + macOS 20 (smoke only); no Windows in PR CI (Windows only in nightly); (7) docs.yml gate runs pnpm quality:docs-skills but this only checks required docs existence, skill frontmatter, guide routing integrity, command examples -- no broken-link check or cross-ref validation; (8) marketplace.json (root) and .claude-plugin/marketplace.json both only list pm-claude; pm-codex is Codex-native install only (no marketplace listing needed there); (9) PR template checklist is comprehensive and good quality.

value.author: audit-docs-agent
value.text: DOCS AUDIT 2026-05-31: (1) All core docs/ files are well-structured with Agent Quick Context headers and progressive disclosure; docs-skills gate (quality:docs-skills) validates existence, skill frontmatter, and guide routing but does NOT check for broken cross-links. (2) changelog classifyItem two-tier logic (strong=type/tags vs weak=title) is sound and has good flag-stripping regex, but is ENTIRELY UNDOCUMENTED for contributors — the only source of truth is pm-changelog/src/generator.ts. (3) README.md has CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN inline install mention but no table row in 'Start Here'; docs/README.md 'Read Path' table has no 'Claude Code user' row despite CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN.md being 202 lines. (4) docs/ still contains 'Tracked documentation work: pm-1sb2/pm-3042/pm-r9gu' headers — all three items are CLOSED — these are cosmetic-only stale references (links still resolve to valid .toon files). (5) CODEX_PLUGIN.md (33 lines) is intentionally thin — purely a router to plugins/pm-codex/README.md. This is by design (pm-d97r single-source decision). No gap here. (6) marketplace.json having only pm-claude is correct — pm-codex is Codex-local install only (codex plugin marketplace add .), not Claude Code marketplace-distributed. (7) PR template is high quality; the only minor gap is no check for 'AGENTS.md guidance updated if new agent-relevant commands shipped'. (8) Windows is deliberately nightly-only (cost/time); PR matrix is Ubuntu+macOS Node 20 only by design (pm-lkd7 decision). (9) No ONBOARDING.md exists and no existing closed item covers creating one — genuine forward gap.

value.author: audit-docs-agent
value.text: Progress 3/3: Audit complete. Dedup checks done for all proposed items via pm search. No existing open items found covering: (A) CHANGELOG classifier documentation for contributors, (B) README/docs Read Path table Claude Code user row, (C) stale tracker-item header cleanup across docs/, (D) docs broken-link CI check, (E) PR template AGENTS.md guidance checkbox. Proposed 6 forward items + 3 ADRs + 2 stories. All items are net-new with no existing closed/open duplicates found in pm search. Leaving pm-obxz in_progress as instructed.

patch.value: Domain audit complete; findings synthesized into the forward living map (open backlog) and ADR log; no duplicates created.
Verification task completed during living-map closeout

The closed Task 'Audit domain: Docs, onboarding, release, changelog & CI' completed its verification pass and left no duplicate or hierarchy blocker for finalization.
